sections.title: Intake
sections.conclusion: Baseline fields and source anchors establish whether the proposal can be reviewed at all.

sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: yes
Requested: Plutus V3 Cost Model Parameter Changes Prior to Chang 2
Stated amount: not stated in document

Deliverables: We propose to update the Plutus V3 Cost Model via a Parameter Update governance action to enable the new Plutus primitives that will be available following the Chang 2 hard fork (to Protocol Version 10)., Existing Plutus cost model settings will not be changed., Note that the primitives will not be enabled in Protocol version 9 (as enabled by the Chang hard fork)., Following the hard fork to Protocol Version 10, 12 new Plutus primitives will be available for bitwise and cryptographic operations.

Claim (technical, independently_verifiable, high materiality): We propose to update the Plutus V3 Cost Model via a Parameter Update governance action to enable the new Plutus primitives that will be available following the Chang 2 hard fork (to Protocol Version 10).
Claim (technical, independently_verifiable, low materiality): Existing Plutus cost model settings will not be changed.
Claim (technical, independently_verifiable, high materiality): Note that the primitives will not be enabled in Protocol version 9 (as enabled by the Chang hard fork).
Claim (technical, independently_verifiable, high materiality): Following the hard fork to Protocol Version 10, 12 new Plutus primitives will be available for bitwise and cryptographic operations.
Claim (technical, independently_verifiable, high materiality): This governance action provides cost model settings so that these primitives can be used following the Chang 2 hard fork.
Claim (technical, independently_verifiable, high materiality): Note that the new primitives will not be enabled in Protocol version 9 (as enabled by the Chang hard fork).
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

sections.title: Risk review
sections.conclusion: Risk is not a side note; unmitigated execution or governance risk can dominate an otherwise attractive proposal.

sections.title: Counterargument pass
findings: Strongest YES: the proposal substantiates "We propose to update the Plutus V3 Cost Model via a Parameter Update governance action to enable the new Plutus primitives that will be available following the Chang 2 hard fork (to Protocol Version 10)." and clears the evidence gates.
Strongest NO: weak controls, unclear delivery, or governance precedent could outweigh the benefit of ParameterChange.
Strongest hold: if claims cannot be tied to replayable evidence, abstaining avoids overclaiming certainty.
sections.conclusion: A defensible rationale must show the best opposing case before it reaches a vote.

sections.title: Synthesis
findings: Final vote must be derived from completed sections above, not from a prose summary.
The public rationale should name the decisive section and the strongest counterargument.
sections.conclusion: The vote is only credible if the assessment tree shows enough work for a skeptical delegator to audit.
